name: Atea Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
business: Atea Pharmaceuticals is a late-stage clinical biopharmaceutical company discovering, developing and commercializing novel, orally administered antivirals to treat serious viral diseases, with lead product candidates including the regimen of bemnifosbuvir and ruzasvir for hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection and AT-587 for hepatitis E virus (HEV) infection.
sellsTo: physicians and clinics treating viral infections
namedSuppliers: MSD International GmbH, an affiliate of Merck & Co., Inc.

exposures.sentence: Our current product candidate pipeline includes the regimen of bemnifosbuvir and ruzasvir which we believe has the potential to improve the current standard of care (SOC) for the treatment of patients with hepatitis C virus (HCV) infection and AT-587 which we believe has the potential to be the first direct-acting antiviral (DAA) for the treatment of patients, particularly immunocompromised patients, with chronic hepatitis E virus (HEV) infection.

catalysts.event: Report topline results from C-BEYOND Phase 3 trial
catalysts.sentence: C-BEYOND is fully enrolled with over 880 patients, and we expect to report topline results mid-2026.

catalysts.event: Report topline results from C-FORWARD Phase 3 trial
catalysts.sentence: We are actively progressing enrollment of an additional 880 patients in C-FORWARD and expect to report topline results from C-FORWARD at year-end 2026.

catalysts.event: Submit NDA to FDA for HCV product candidate
catalysts.sentence: Pending successful results from these Phase 3 clinical trials, we are targeting submission to US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) of a New Drug Application (NDA) for marketing approval in March 2027.

catalysts.event: Initiate first-in-human Phase 1 study for AT-587
catalysts.sentence: Currently, we anticipate initiating clinical development of AT-587 with a first-in-human Phase 1 study in mid-2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global net sales of branded HCV therapeutics exceeded $2.5 billion in 2025, with US accounting for approximately 50%.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2025, global net sales of branded HCV therapeutics known in the US as Epclusa (including the authorized generic copy of Epclusa) and Mavyret exceeded $2.5 billion with the US accounting for approximately 50% of these sales.

tamClaims.claim: Up to 4 million persons in the US infected with HCV, with 115 newly reported chronic infections per year.
tamClaims.sentence: Given the large number of patients currently infected with HCV, which is reported by the US Center for Disease Control and Prevention to be as many as four million persons in the US, and the incidence of 115 newly reported chronic infections continuing to outpace rates of treatment, we expect that a substantial global market will exist for the foreseeable future.

tamClaims.claim: No approved therapies for HEV, representing a substantial unmet medical need.
tamClaims.sentence: There are no DAAs currently available for the treatment of HEV.
